summary:
There is insufficient direct evidence showing a causal relationship between Omega-3 Index levels and reduced mortality from coronary heart disease; while associated with healthy diets such as the Mediterranean diet, its independent effect remains unproven due to lack of robust randomized controlled trial support. 

The statement "Omega-3 index is a new risk factor" lacks sufficient supporting data because current studies do not establish an independent protective association against cardiac deaths through measurable changes in blood-based Omega-3 concentrations alone.

Final Answer:

\boxed{\text{Disagree}}

Okay, I need to summarize only what Agent2 reasoned about this specific question regarding whether the Omega-3 Index serves as a new risk factor for death from coronary heart disease.

First sentence should capture their core finding based directly on retrieval results—no strong proof connecting Omega-3 Index specifically to lower CHD-related mortality—even if linked indirectly via lifestyle factors like Mediterranean eating habits.

Second sentence must highlight why they disagree—the absence of high-quality RCTs proving benefit independently makes it inaccurate to call it “a new” established risk factor.

I will keep both under two full sentences strictly focused on clinical relevance without extra commentary.
